_meta.description: Employer-calibrated cohort rubrics grounded in real job postings, hiring articles, and recruiter writeups. Each signal cites the source it came from. This file is consumed by dilly_core/rubric_scorer.py. It does NOT replace dilly_core/cohort_scoring_weights.py or dilly_core/auditor.py : it runs in parallel as an opt-in scoring path.
_meta.editing_notes: Every signal MUST have a 'detector' field to be counted. Signals without detectors are listed as 'unmeasured' and do not affect the score (MTS principle : only detected evidence counts). If you change weights or add signals, run tests/golden_resumes_test.py to compare before and after.
_meta.detector_dsl_reference: See dilly_core/rubric_scorer.py top docstring for the full detector DSL. Quick reference: {type: 'field'|'major_in'|'regex'|'keyword_any'|'keyword_all'|'composite', ...}

tech_software_engineering.dimension_weights_reasoning: Sources unanimous: demonstrated building > academic signals. Nucamp cites 60% of recruiters want real-world projects, 80% of top employers want clean documented code. Medium: 'A 9.5 GPA with no practical experience loses out to a 6.5 GPA with real-world projects on GitHub.' Build dominates at 0.50. Smart still gates (GPA filters exist at large companies) and grit captures contribution consistency.
tech_software_engineering.recruiter_bar_reasoning: Calibrated estimate. No source gives a numeric bar. 70/100 leaves headroom above it for top-tier candidates (who'd score 85+) while being reachable by students with 3-5 deep projects and a clean GitHub.

high_impact.signal: Baseline cohort fit : student is in a major relevant to this cohort
high_impact.rationale: Every student who chose a major in this cohort's field gets baseline credit for being in the right place. This prevents the scoring formula from crushing students who match the rubric's MAIN signals but miss rare/exceptional ones (CFA, MCAT, OSCP, Kaggle Expert, etc.). It encodes the simple truth that being in the right field is itself a real signal.

high_impact.signal: GPA 3.5+ in CS or quantitative major
high_impact.rationale: Careerflow recommends listing GPA only if 3.5+. Acts as a filter, not a differentiator.

medium_impact.signal: Relevant CS coursework listed (DSA, systems, distributed, ML)
medium_impact.rationale: Careerflow recommends listing 'relevant coursework that directly aligns with the jobs you're targeting' for candidates with limited work experience.
Careerflow: proactive learning signal, secondary to projects.

low_impact.signal: Listing 'proficient in N programming languages' without outcome context
low_impact.rationale: FinalRoundAI + tops-int: recruiters treat skill lists as claims that GitHub must back up. Actively neutral.

high_impact.signal: Baseline cohort fit : work experience in a relevant major
high_impact.rationale: Students in the right major with real work experience get baseline grit credit. Having worked in the field (even without field-specific role keywords) is a real signal of commitment. This prevents students from scoring 0 on grit just because the rubric's specific role keywords don't match their resume language.

high_impact.signal: GitHub link on resume (baseline signal : hire tool can't verify commit graph)
high_impact.rationale: FinalRoundAI cites 78% of tech recruiters check GitHub before scheduling interviews. Without a link they can't check consistency.

high_impact.signal: Deployed application with live URL
high_impact.rationale: Nucamp emphasizes measurable impact; a live deployment proves the project actually works end-to-end.

medium_impact.rationale: Finalround: hackathons signal sustained effort and ability to ship under pressure.

medium_impact.signal: Recognized tech employer on work history (Stripe, Google, Meta, etc.)
medium_impact.rationale: Ramp: 'builders who ship' philosophy confirmed across top tech employer hiring. Prior brand-name experience is a strong grit signal because these companies don't hire students who don't execute.

medium_impact.signal: Competitive programming (LeetCode, Codeforces, ICPC, HackerRank ranked)
medium_impact.rationale: Demonstrates sustained algorithmic practice : relevant for technical interview prep.

high_impact.signal: Baseline cohort fit : relevant coursework/project language detected
high_impact.rationale: Students in the right major whose resume uses field-specific language (tools, techniques, projects) get baseline build credit. This rewards 'having done something in the field' even if the specific high-impact build signals (publications, portfolios, certifications) aren't present.

high_impact.signal: Tech projects with quantified outcomes in bullets
high_impact.rationale: Nucamp: '60% of recruiters specifically seek projects that tackle real-world problems.' Careerflow demands quantified results in place of vague claims.

high_impact.signal: Deployed/hosted project (signal overlap with grit but also build quality)
high_impact.rationale: Nucamp: live deployment is the cheapest way to prove a project works end-to-end.

medium_impact.signal: At least one of Python / JavaScript / TypeScript / Go / Rust / Java mentioned with context
medium_impact.rationale: Variety across stack: Nucamp notes 70% of hiring managers want variety.

low_impact.signal: Listed tech stack without any project or outcome
low_impact.rationale: FinalRoundAI explicitly: recruiters view GitHub as proof; skills without projects are discounted.

common_rejection_reasons: No GitHub link on resume, or GitHub contains only forks and course homework
Generic non-tailored resume : one-size-fits-all doesn't pass ATS
Skills lists with no project evidence to back them up
Vague achievement bullets ('good at teamwork') instead of quantified outcomes
Tutorial clones (Todo app, weather app) presented as flagship projects
fastest_path_moves: Deploy ONE existing project to a live URL (Vercel, Fly, Render) and pin its repo on GitHub with a real README
Rewrite every experience and project bullet using STAR format + a number
Make 2-3 small open-source contributions to seed the contribution graph
Remove tutorial-clone repos from your public GitHub
Add a one-line GitHub URL to the header of your resume
Tailor the resume per application: mirror the job posting's exact tech stack keywords for ATS

tech_data_science.dimension_weights_reasoning: DS hiring is more credentials-and-fundamentals weighted than SWE. Stripe and Databricks 2026 intern listings explicitly require quantitative degree programs plus named methods (causal inference, GLMs, time series, experimentation). Build is 0.38 because projects on real datasets (NOT Titanic/MNIST) are what move resumes out of the no pile. Grit is lower (0.22) than SWE because DS interns are evaluated more on rigor than on relentless shipping cadence.
tech_data_science.recruiter_bar_reasoning: Top-tier DS roles (Stripe PhD DS, Databricks) explicitly require Master's/PhD, out of reach for most undergrads. Mid-tier (Capital One analyst intern) accepts 3.0+ with SQL + portfolio. 72 reflects bar for interview at median competitive employer.

high_impact.signal: Baseline cohort fit : student is in a major relevant to this cohort
high_impact.rationale: Every student who chose a major in this cohort's field gets baseline credit for being in the right place. This prevents the scoring formula from crushing students who match the rubric's MAIN signals but miss rare/exceptional ones (CFA, MCAT, OSCP, Kaggle Expert, etc.). It encodes the simple truth that being in the right field is itself a real signal.

high_impact.signal: 3.5+ GPA in quantitative major (Stats, Math, CS, Data Science, Physics, Econ, Engineering)
high_impact.rationale: Databricks 2026 DS Intern requires quantitative field; Stripe lists 'Data Science, Statistics, Economics, Mathematics'.

high_impact.signal: Coursework in probability, stats, regression, experimental design, or causal inference
high_impact.rationale: Databricks names 'generalized linear regression, regression and classification trees, unsupervised learning, causal inference, stochastic processes, time series forecasting' as required familiarity.

medium_impact.rationale: Coursera 2026 guide and Capital One posting cite 3.0 as typical floor; clears screen but doesn't differentiate.

low_impact.signal: Standalone Coursera / edX / DataCamp certificates with no project output
low_impact.rationale: KDnuggets explicitly lists 'listing MOOC certificates prominently' as top-5 resume mistake.

high_impact.signal: Baseline cohort fit : work experience in a relevant major
high_impact.rationale: Students in the right major with real work experience get baseline grit credit. Having worked in the field (even without field-specific role keywords) is a real signal of commitment. This prevents students from scoring 0 on grit just because the rubric's specific role keywords don't match their resume language.

high_impact.signal: Prior data / research / analyst internship or RA position
high_impact.rationale: 365DataScience and Forage rank prior real-stakes data work as highest non-academic signal.

high_impact.signal: Kaggle participation (competitions, datasets, notebooks)
high_impact.rationale: Scaler and Coursera name Kaggle as verifiable signal of sustained effort and competitive benchmarking.

medium_impact.signal: GitHub link indicating sustained data work
medium_impact.rationale: Scaler: 'most hiring managers look at your GitHub profile'.

low_impact.signal: Generic club membership with no output
low_impact.rationale: KDnuggets: resume filler that doesn't tie to a deliverable.

high_impact.signal: Baseline cohort fit : relevant coursework/project language detected
high_impact.rationale: Students in the right major whose resume uses field-specific language (tools, techniques, projects) get baseline build credit. This rewards 'having done something in the field' even if the specific high-impact build signals (publications, portfolios, certifications) aren't present.

high_impact.signal: SQL fluency mentioned with project context
high_impact.rationale: Every DS job posting reviewed lists SQL as required. For analyst track it's THE skill.

high_impact.signal: Python fluency with pandas / numpy / scikit-learn / ML libs
high_impact.rationale: Databricks: 'proven ability to work with data in SQL and Python'. Stripe: 'scientific computing language'.

high_impact.signal: Projects with quantified data outcomes
high_impact.rationale: KDnuggets + Dataquest: absence of quantified outcomes is the #1 rejection reason.

medium_impact.signal: Data visualization / BI tool mentioned (Tableau, PowerBI, Looker, Streamlit)
medium_impact.rationale: Capital One DA Intern posting names Tableau-style work; 365DS confirms BI tools are core for analyst track.

low_impact.signal: Toy datasets as flagship projects (Titanic, MNIST, Iris, Boston Housing)
low_impact.rationale: KDnuggets: 'hard to think of a faster way to have your resume thrown into the definite no pile'.

common_rejection_reasons: Project section is only Titanic / MNIST / Iris : 'faster way to the no pile'
Stack of MOOC certificates with no portfolio output
No quantified results : bullets say 'analyzed data' with no metric
No SQL evidence anywhere on resume (instant filter for analyst-track)
Skill list mentions ML libraries but projects show only EDA / pandas
fastest_path_moves: Replace any Titanic/MNIST project with one project on a real messy dataset, write up methodology + result with a number
Get to Kaggle Expert tier (one bronze medal in a real competition)
Build one SQL-heavy project with window functions and CTEs on a real schema
Write one A/B test analysis with hypothesis, power calc, and decision recommendation
Find a research assistantship with any prof who touches data

business_finance.dimension_weights_reasoning: Finance recruiting weights academic + analytical signal heavily. M&I: sub-3.5 is 'low', sub-3.0 auto-reject at BBs. Grit captures the non-target hustle path (200-500 banker conversations, certifications). Build is lower because real finance 'builds' (DCFs, stock pitches) are scarcer and harder to verify on a resume.
business_finance.recruiter_bar_reasoning: Finance has a documented hard floor. JPM IB requires 3.2 minimum and 3.5+ to be competitive. Goldman safe zone starts at 3.5, elite groups want 3.6+.

high_impact.signal: Baseline cohort fit : student is in a major relevant to this cohort
high_impact.rationale: Every student who chose a major in this cohort's field gets baseline credit for being in the right place. This prevents the scoring formula from crushing students who match the rubric's MAIN signals but miss rare/exceptional ones (CFA, MCAT, OSCP, Kaggle Expert, etc.). It encodes the simple truth that being in the right field is itself a real signal.

high_impact.signal: 3.7+ GPA in Finance / Accounting / Economics / quantitative major
high_impact.rationale: M&I states sub-3.5 is 'low', sub-3.0 triggers auto-reject. ibinterviewquestions: aim for 3.7+ from non-targets.

high_impact.signal: CFA Level 1 passed or registered/sat
high_impact.rationale: M&I: CFA has 'genuine value' for portfolio management, asset management, equity research. Strong verifiable signal of quantitative effort from non-targets.

medium_impact.signal: 3.5 to 3.69 GPA in a finance-adjacent major
medium_impact.rationale: Goldman 'safe zone' starts at 3.5.

medium_impact.signal: Bloomberg Market Concepts (BMC) or Wall Street Prep / Training the Street / BIWS certification
medium_impact.rationale: ibinterviewquestions lists these as substitutes for formal training candidates missed.

low_impact.rationale: M&I: Excel is assumed; listing it is resume theater.

high_impact.signal: Baseline cohort fit : work experience in a relevant major
high_impact.rationale: Students in the right major with real work experience get baseline grit credit. Having worked in the field (even without field-specific role keywords) is a real signal of commitment. This prevents students from scoring 0 on grit just because the rubric's specific role keywords don't match their resume language.

high_impact.signal: Prior finance-adjacent internship (wealth management, corporate finance, Big 4 transaction advisory, equity research, regional bank)
high_impact.rationale: ibinterviewquestions: 'Big Four transaction advisory, corporate development, equity research' are the non-target stepping-stone ladder.

high_impact.signal: Leadership in investment club, finance society, student-run fund
high_impact.rationale: Non-target playbook: student-run fund leadership is high-impact grit signal.

medium_impact.rationale: Finance recruiters use honors as a filter when GPA is borderline.

low_impact.rationale: M&I: recruiters interpret soft-skill claims unfavorably or ignore them.

high_impact.signal: Baseline cohort fit : relevant coursework/project language detected
high_impact.rationale: Students in the right major whose resume uses field-specific language (tools, techniques, projects) get baseline build credit. This rewards 'having done something in the field' even if the specific high-impact build signals (publications, portfolios, certifications) aren't present.

high_impact.signal: Financial modeling project (DCF, LBO, merger model) mentioned on resume
high_impact.rationale: ibinterviewquestions: modeling proficiency is 'non-negotiable'. A candidate who can walk through a self-built model signals exactly what an analyst job requires day one.

high_impact.signal: Stock pitch, equity research writeup, or investment thesis presented/published
high_impact.rationale: ibinterviewquestions: stock pitches are universal currency for ER, AM, HF interviews; BB IBD interviewers ask regularly.

high_impact.signal: Finance case competition participation (CFA Research Challenge, Rotman International Trading, KWHS, McIntire, Cornell ICIC)
high_impact.rationale: ibinterviewquestions: case competitions are verifiable, externally-judged builds.

medium_impact.rationale: Differentiator signal : most undergrads never touch a terminal.

low_impact.signal: Listing 'built a DCF in class' with no detail or link
low_impact.rationale: Class assignments are floor-level expectation; without depth they're noise.

common_rejection_reasons: GPA below 3.5 with no upward trend or compensating signal (CFA, top finance grades, modeling depth)
Non-target school + zero networking record + zero finance internships
All 'finance interest' and zero finance proof (no club leadership, no model, no pitch, no internship)
Generic 'proficient in Excel/PowerPoint/Bloomberg' as skills with nothing built
Applied late in cycle : Goldman fills most seats by November prior
CFA L1 listed without GPA support : CFA cannot rescue a sub-3.0 GPA
fastest_path_moves: Build a real DCF on a public company you understand, write a 1-page investment memo, practice walking through it in 90 seconds
Register for CFA Level 1 : even pre-pass, registration is a credible signal for ER/AM/buy-side
Complete Bloomberg Market Concepts (BMC) : ~8 hours, ~$150, instantly verifiable credential
Run a 100-conversation networking sprint targeting UTampa alumni and Florida/regional finance firms
Apply to spring 2026 diversity summits (Goldman Possibilities, JPM Advancing Black Pathways, MS Early Insights)
Take president/VP/Investments Chair in the UTampa investment club within one year
Lock in stepping-stone internship: Big 4 transaction advisory, regional wealth management, corporate finance
business_finance.utampa_specific_notes: UTampa is non-target for BBs/EBs. The recruiting model requires: (1) higher GPA bar (3.7+ not 3.5) because of the non-target tax; (2) 200-500 networking conversations as substitute for on-campus recruiting; (3) Florida-specific opportunities: Raymond James (St. Pete), Jefferies Miami, Citadel (Miami HQ since 2022), Point72 Miami, Goldman West Palm Beach; (4) Tampa corporate finance at Jabil, Bloomin' Brands, Mosaic; (5) diversity programs as explicit non-target on-ramps. Rubric rewards the compensating signals (CFA, modeling depth, club leadership, regional internships) that make the non-target playbook work.

pre_health.display_name: Pre-Health (Med / PA / Dental / Vet / PT / Pharm)
pre_health.source_verification_note: Canonical URLs cited from knowledge because WebFetch was denied. All URLs are real AAMC/AACOM/PAEA/ADEA/AAVMC pages.
pre_health.dimension_weights_reasoning: Pre-health is the most academics-dominant cohort. AAMC matriculant data shows MCAT + BCPM GPA are strongest predictors. Grit captures longitudinal clinical hours, shadowing, resilience. Build is weighted lowest because pre-health isn't judged on shipped artifacts : only research output.
pre_health.recruiter_bar_reasoning: Median MD matriculant MCAT ~511, median cGPA ~3.77, BCPM ~3.70 per AAMC FACTS A-16. PA programs require ~1,000-2,000 patient-care hours. 80 reflects realistic admissible bar.

high_impact.signal: Baseline cohort fit : student is in a major relevant to this cohort
high_impact.rationale: Every student who chose a major in this cohort's field gets baseline credit for being in the right place. This prevents the scoring formula from crushing students who match the rubric's MAIN signals but miss rare/exceptional ones (CFA, MCAT, OSCP, Kaggle Expert, etc.). It encodes the simple truth that being in the right field is itself a real signal.

high_impact.signal: Cumulative GPA 3.7+ (matriculant median 3.77)
high_impact.rationale: AAMC matriculant median cGPA is 3.77.

high_impact.signal: BCPM GPA 3.6+ (field is separately extracted from transcript by dilly_core)
high_impact.rationale: Adcoms compute BCPM separately and weight above cumulative. dilly_core extracts bcpm_gpa when present.

high_impact.signal: MCAT mentioned on resume
high_impact.rationale: MCAT is THE dominant smart signal for med school. Detection is coarse (any mention) because resumes often don't publish the score, but mention suggests readiness.

medium_impact.signal: Strong grades in upper-level science (biochem, genetics, physiology, anatomy)
medium_impact.rationale: AAMC explicitly notes upper-level science grades as med-school readiness proxies.

medium_impact.signal: Pre-med major (Biochemistry, Biomedical Sciences, Biology) with any honors signal
medium_impact.rationale: Combination of major fit and recognition.

low_impact.signal: Non-science cumulative GPA (cGPA matters but BCPM dominates)
low_impact.rationale: AAMC: BCPM dominates; cGPA is secondary.

high_impact.signal: Baseline cohort fit : work experience in a relevant major
high_impact.rationale: Students in the right major with real work experience get baseline grit credit. Having worked in the field (even without field-specific role keywords) is a real signal of commitment. This prevents students from scoring 0 on grit just because the rubric's specific role keywords don't match their resume language.

high_impact.signal: Longitudinal clinical experience (research semesters >= 2 as proxy)
high_impact.rationale: PAEA/AAMC emphasize sustained clinical exposure. longitudinal_clinical_years field in dilly_core tracks this.

high_impact.signal: Clinical role mentioned: scribe, EMT, CNA, MA, phlebotomist, ED tech
high_impact.rationale: Paid clinical roles signal commitment AND competence.

high_impact.signal: Physician shadowing mentioned
high_impact.rationale: Shadowing is a baseline expectation for MD applicants. DO schools explicitly require DO shadowing.

medium_impact.signal: Leadership in healthcare-related student org (AMSA, Pre-PA, Red Cross)
medium_impact.rationale: Student-org leadership in healthcare context signals sustained interest.

medium_impact.signal: Sustained non-clinical service (food bank, tutoring underserved, crisis line)
medium_impact.rationale: AAMC Core Competencies include Service Orientation.

low_impact.signal: One-off mission trips (flagged as 'voluntourism' by adcoms)
low_impact.rationale: Adcoms note negatively when mission trips are the only service signal.

high_impact.signal: Baseline cohort fit : relevant coursework/project language detected
high_impact.rationale: Students in the right major whose resume uses field-specific language (tools, techniques, projects) get baseline build credit. This rewards 'having done something in the field' even if the specific high-impact build signals (publications, portfolios, certifications) aren't present.

high_impact.signal: Research experience with longevity (research_longevity_years >= 1)
high_impact.rationale: Multi-semester research with same PI is the primary build signal for pre-health.

high_impact.signal: Any research mentioned (extracted by dilly_core as has_research boolean)
high_impact.rationale: Any research exposure is a meaningful signal for pre-health, even short-term.

high_impact.signal: Publication or conference presentation mentioned
high_impact.rationale: PubMed-indexed publications or conference posters are the highest build signal.

medium_impact.rationale: Signals independent scientific thought beyond coursework.

common_rejection_reasons: GPA below 3.5 (cumulative or BCPM) : below the matriculant median floor
No longitudinal clinical hours : shows you haven't confirmed you want medicine
Generic pre-med club membership with no leadership or deliverable
One-off mission trips as the only service signal (voluntourism flag)
No research at all for MD/PhD or research-focused programs
fastest_path_moves: Lock in one paid clinical role (scribe, EMT, CNA, MA) and commit to 12+ months
Find a research lab and stay 2+ years : longevity beats volume
Document physician shadowing with named MDs and specialties (50+ hours)
Sustain one non-clinical service commitment for 1+ year (food bank, tutoring)
If GPA is shaky: start strong in upper-level science to establish upward trend
Begin MCAT prep 3-6 months out; target 510+ for reach programs

business_marketing.source_verification_note: Canonical URLs cited from knowledge; WebFetch was denied for many.
business_marketing.dimension_weights_reasoning: Marketing is dramatically more build-heavy than finance/consulting because portfolio beats pedigree. CPG brand track is slightly more academic (smart +0.05) while creator/growth tracks are nearly pure build. We pick build-heavy defaults; GPA matters less.
business_marketing.recruiter_bar_reasoning: Marketing is an entry-accessible field where a strong portfolio can carry a weaker GPA. Bar is lower than finance/consulting because the path doesn't require prestige credentials.

high_impact.signal: Baseline cohort fit : student is in a major relevant to this cohort
high_impact.rationale: Every student who chose a major in this cohort's field gets baseline credit for being in the right place. This prevents the scoring formula from crushing students who match the rubric's MAIN signals but miss rare/exceptional ones (CFA, MCAT, OSCP, Kaggle Expert, etc.). It encodes the simple truth that being in the right field is itself a real signal.

high_impact.signal: GPA 3.5+ in marketing / business / communications / data science
high_impact.rationale: CPG brand track (P&G, Unilever) uses GPA as a filter.

high_impact.signal: Google Analytics / HubSpot / Meta Blueprint / Google Ads certification
high_impact.rationale: Demonstrable analytics literacy : a common filter for digital/growth track roles.

medium_impact.signal: Coursework in stats, marketing research, consumer behavior, econometrics
medium_impact.rationale: Analytical coursework signals CPG brand and tech PMM readiness.

low_impact.signal: Generic 'Excel proficient' claim without analytics artifact
low_impact.rationale: Excel is assumed; listing it is resume theater.

high_impact.signal: Baseline cohort fit : work experience in a relevant major
high_impact.rationale: Students in the right major with real work experience get baseline grit credit. Having worked in the field (even without field-specific role keywords) is a real signal of commitment. This prevents students from scoring 0 on grit just because the rubric's specific role keywords don't match their resume language.

high_impact.signal: Prior marketing-adjacent work (any paid marketing/comms role)
high_impact.rationale: A prior real-stakes marketing role is the strongest grit signal.

high_impact.signal: Leadership in marketing club, AMA chapter, business frat marketing committee
high_impact.rationale: Marketing club leadership is a realistic UTampa-level signal.

medium_impact.signal: Social media with documented growth or engagement
medium_impact.rationale: Running an org's social with a growth number is a real portfolio signal.

high_impact.signal: Baseline cohort fit : relevant coursework/project language detected
high_impact.rationale: Students in the right major whose resume uses field-specific language (tools, techniques, projects) get baseline build credit. This rewards 'having done something in the field' even if the specific high-impact build signals (publications, portfolios, certifications) aren't present.

high_impact.signal: Campaign artifacts with quantified outcomes
high_impact.rationale: Live portfolio with metrics (grew X from 0 to 50k, +Y% conversion) is the strongest build signal.

high_impact.signal: Portfolio, case study, or deployed personal brand site
high_impact.rationale: Live URL to case studies or personal brand work : build dominance.

high_impact.signal: Case competition participation (L'Oreal Brandstorm, NABC, Unilever Future Leaders League)
high_impact.rationale: Competitive marketing case work is a verifiable build signal.

medium_impact.signal: Visual / creative tools mentioned (Figma, Canva, Adobe Creative Cloud, Photoshop, Illustrator)
medium_impact.rationale: Creative tooling for content/brand tracks.

low_impact.signal: Class project deck with no real audience or data
low_impact.rationale: Class projects without real metrics are floor-level.

common_rejection_reasons: No portfolio link on resume : marketing is portfolio-first
Social media claims without engagement or growth numbers
Class projects as flagship work with no real audience
Missing analytics literacy (no GA4, HubSpot, or dashboard tool)
Generic 'social media marketing' bullets with no brand or metric
fastest_path_moves: Build a portfolio site with 3 case studies: problem → strategy → execution → result (with numbers)
Run a real $50-100 paid ad campaign (Meta or Google) and report CPA/ROAS
Take over social media for ONE student org, document 6-month growth numbers
Write an A/B test analysis: hypothesis, experiment, result, decision
Get Google Analytics (GA4) + HubSpot Inbound certified (~8 hours total)
Apply to Unilever Future Leaders League or L'Oreal Brandstorm : case comp trophies are portfolio gold

business_accounting.source_verification_note: Canonical URLs cited from knowledge; WebFetch was denied.
business_accounting.dimension_weights_reasoning: Accounting is GPA/credentials-heavy but less so than finance. CPA trajectory (150 credit hours + exam readiness) is critical. Build captures Excel/analytics tools.
business_accounting.recruiter_bar_reasoning: Big 4 typically hires at 3.3+ GPA with 150-hour path. MAcc students are preferred. 72 reflects realistic Big 4 bar.

high_impact.signal: Baseline cohort fit : student is in a major relevant to this cohort
high_impact.rationale: Every student who chose a major in this cohort's field gets baseline credit for being in the right place. This prevents the scoring formula from crushing students who match the rubric's MAIN signals but miss rare/exceptional ones (CFA, MCAT, OSCP, Kaggle Expert, etc.). It encodes the simple truth that being in the right field is itself a real signal.

high_impact.signal: GPA 3.5+ in Accounting
high_impact.rationale: Big 4 uses GPA as a filter; 3.5+ is the competitive threshold.

high_impact.signal: Clear 150-credit-hour plan (MAcc, dual major, or 5-year program) mentioned
high_impact.rationale: CPA eligibility requires 150 hours; stating your path signals readiness.

high_impact.signal: CPA exam sections mentioned or in progress
high_impact.rationale: A started CPA exam is a strong commitment signal.

medium_impact.signal: Dean's List / Honors in accounting coursework
medium_impact.rationale: Accounting recruiters weight honors when GPA is borderline.

medium_impact.signal: Coursework in Intermediate Accounting, Audit, Tax, AIS
medium_impact.rationale: Upper-level accounting coursework signals preparedness.

high_impact.signal: Baseline cohort fit : work experience in a relevant major
high_impact.rationale: Students in the right major with real work experience get baseline grit credit. Having worked in the field (even without field-specific role keywords) is a real signal of commitment. This prevents students from scoring 0 on grit just because the rubric's specific role keywords don't match their resume language.

high_impact.signal: Beta Alpha Psi member with officer role
high_impact.rationale: BAP is the national accounting honor society : officer role is a top signal.

high_impact.signal: VITA (Volunteer Income Tax Assistance) volunteer : IRS certified
high_impact.rationale: VITA is a real-stakes tax role; IRS certification signals readiness.

high_impact.signal: Prior accounting internship (local/regional firm)
high_impact.rationale: Stepping-stone internship before Big 4 is the standard non-target ladder.

medium_impact.signal: Accounting Society / Student Accounting Association leadership
medium_impact.rationale: Club leadership signals sustained interest.

high_impact.signal: Baseline cohort fit : relevant coursework/project language detected
high_impact.rationale: Students in the right major whose resume uses field-specific language (tools, techniques, projects) get baseline build credit. This rewards 'having done something in the field' even if the specific high-impact build signals (publications, portfolios, certifications) aren't present.

high_impact.signal: Data analytics tools for accounting: Alteryx, Tableau, Power BI
high_impact.rationale: Data analytics fluency is increasingly demanded at Big 4 : core differentiator.

high_impact.signal: Advanced Excel: pivot tables, VLOOKUP, INDEX/MATCH, Power Query
high_impact.rationale: Excel fluency beyond basics is a real differentiator.

medium_impact.signal: ERP / accounting software exposure (SAP, Oracle, NetSuite, QuickBooks)
medium_impact.rationale: Software exposure signals ability to work with real client systems.

common_rejection_reasons: GPA below 3.3 with no 150-hour plan : can't be CPA eligible
No accounting-specific experience (no internship, no VITA, no bookkeeping)
Only basic Excel without advanced features
No Big 4 leadership/discover program attendance
Missing Beta Alpha Psi or accounting society leadership
fastest_path_moves: Commit to a 150-hour path: MAcc, dual major, or 5-year program : state it on resume
Join Beta Alpha Psi and target an officer role within 1 year
Volunteer with VITA next tax season : IRS certification + real returns
Start Becker or Wiley CPA review in senior year; pass 1 section before graduation
Build one Alteryx or Tableau project on a real financial dataset
Apply to Deloitte National Leadership / PwC Start / EY Discover / KPMG Ignite

tech_cybersecurity.source_verification_note: Knowledge-based. Cybersecurity hiring is one of the most certification-driven cohorts : the signals here are well-established industry standards.
tech_cybersecurity.dimension_weights_reasoning: Cyber hiring weights demonstrated hands-on work (labs, CTFs, home labs) heavily. Certs are the 'smart' signal because entry-level cyber is cert-driven. Grit captures sustained CTF play and lab maintenance.
tech_cybersecurity.recruiter_bar_reasoning: Entry-level cyber (SOC analyst intern, junior analyst) is one of the most accessible tech paths. The bar is lower than FAANG SWE because many good cyber analysts don't come from CS : they come from certifications + hands-on work.

high_impact.rationale: Home labs signal self-directed building beyond coursework.

Add a scripted tool to GitHub: log parser, port scanner, or phishing detection script

high_impact.signal: Case competition finalist / participant (MBB cases, Net Impact, Deloitte Nat'l Undergrad)
high_impact.rationale: Externally-judged case work is the top build signal.

high_impact.signal: Quantified leadership outcomes in experience bullets
high_impact.rationale: Measurable impact in any leadership context is what consulting recruiters look for.

medium_impact.rationale: Research signals structured thinking.

common_rejection_reasons: GPA below 3.5 : hard floor at most top firms
No case interview preparation signal : fatal
No consulting club or strategy-related leadership
Generic business experience without quantified outcomes
Missing international / language / travel signals for global firms
fastest_path_moves: Start Case in Point + begin case partners : 100+ cases before interviews
Take a leadership role in a consulting or business club
Apply to MBB diversity programs (Sophomore Summit, ADC, BEAM)
Target a stepping stone: Deloitte Strategy, EY Parthenon, Kearney, or corporate strategy intern
Enter case competitions : even participation matters
Learn basic Excel modeling and PowerPoint deck building

pre_law.source_verification_note: Canonical URLs cited from knowledge; WebFetch was denied.
pre_law.dimension_weights_reasoning: Pre-law is dominated by LSAT + GPA. Grit captures work experience and pre-law club leadership. Build captures writing output and legal experience.
pre_law.recruiter_bar_reasoning: T14 law schools want 3.8+ GPA and 170+ LSAT. Strong regional schools 3.5+ and 165+.

high_impact.signal: Baseline cohort fit : student is in a major relevant to this cohort
high_impact.rationale: Every student who chose a major in this cohort's field gets baseline credit for being in the right place. This prevents the scoring formula from crushing students who match the rubric's MAIN signals but miss rare/exceptional ones (CFA, MCAT, OSCP, Kaggle Expert, etc.). It encodes the simple truth that being in the right field is itself a real signal.

high_impact.signal: 3.8+ GPA (T14 competitive)
high_impact.rationale: Every tenth of a GPA point matters for law school admissions.

high_impact.signal: LSAT mentioned on resume (score or prep)
high_impact.rationale: LSAT is the dominant smart signal. Mention indicates readiness.

high_impact.signal: Pre-law or rigorous major (Philosophy, Political Science, History, English, Economics)
high_impact.rationale: Traditional pre-law majors signal rhetorical and analytical depth.

medium_impact.rationale: Competitive for strong non-T14 schools.
Academic recognition.

high_impact.signal: Baseline cohort fit : work experience in a relevant major
high_impact.rationale: Students in the right major with real work experience get baseline grit credit. Having worked in the field (even without field-specific role keywords) is a real signal of commitment. This prevents students from scoring 0 on grit just because the rubric's specific role keywords don't match their resume language.

high_impact.signal: Legal experience: law firm, DA's office, public defender, federal judiciary, legal aid
high_impact.rationale: Any exposure to legal work environment is strong grit signal.

high_impact.signal: Moot court, mock trial, debate team
high_impact.rationale: Law school prep activities.

medium_impact.rationale: Student org leadership in law context.

medium_impact.signal: Research assistant to professor (legal or related fields)
medium_impact.rationale: Research experience signals rigor.

high_impact.signal: Baseline cohort fit : relevant coursework/project language detected
high_impact.rationale: Students in the right major whose resume uses field-specific language (tools, techniques, projects) get baseline build credit. This rewards 'having done something in the field' even if the specific high-impact build signals (publications, portfolios, certifications) aren't present.

high_impact.signal: Published writing (student newspaper, law review student note, blog)
high_impact.rationale: Writing ability is the core build signal for pre-law.

high_impact.signal: Senior thesis or honors research with written output
high_impact.rationale: Long-form writing demonstrates analytical depth.

medium_impact.rationale: Capitol Hill, state legislature, campaign work.

common_rejection_reasons: GPA below 3.5 : even strong LSAT struggles to compensate
No LSAT prep signal : suggests not ready to apply
No legal experience : shows lack of commitment
No writing samples or published work : fatal for law school
Missing moot court / mock trial / debate experience
fastest_path_moves: Start LSAT prep NOW : 6+ months before applications
Get an internship at a law firm, DA's office, or legal aid : any legal exposure counts
Join moot court or mock trial; apply to become part of a team
Write regularly for student publication : bylines matter
Take a research assistant role with a professor who writes on legal topics
Consider the writing sample early : it takes multiple drafts
